era-of-embers.note: Final appearance — distinct from Act 1 L4 version. Exhausted, degraded, personal.

biography.value:
Dreyfus, the Shape-Walker — Level 5 Elite

Humanoid (Shape-Walker), Medium

Stamina 64 | Speed 7 | Size M | Stability 2

Dreyfus appears as a grey-pale man with gold unblinking eyes, skin the colour of old ivory. But something is wrong with him. The edges of him flicker, like a candle in a draft. His shifting is less clean than it was — less sure. He carries the specific marks of his fight with the party in Beat 9.

Main Attacks

Talon Strike (Melee, Agility): +4 vs Agility; 9 damage. Target is Slowed (save ends).

Void Pulse (Ranged 8, Presence): +3 vs Intuition; 7 damage. Target has a bane on their next test or attack roll.

Triggered Actions (costs 1 Villain Power)

Shift Form (triggered at start of turn): Dreyfus transforms into a new form. Each form has a different secondary attack:

  • Dire Wolf Form: Pack Lunge — Melee, +4 vs Might; 11 damage, target Prone.
  • Raven Form: Wyrd-Shriek — Close Burst 2, +3 vs Intuition; 5 damage, all targets Dazed until end of next turn. (Speed 10 while in Raven form; flying.)
  • Shadow Form: Drain Warmth — Ranged touch, +3 vs Presence; 8 cold damage, target loses 1 Recovery.

Features

Flickering Body: When Dreyfus takes 10+ damage from a single hit, he immediately teleports 3 squares as a free triggered action.

Wyrd-Read: Dreyfus cannot be surprised. He knows what skills each hero has used so far in the campaign and uses this knowledge tactically.

Shape Collapse: When reduced to 0 Stamina, Dreyfus does not die. He collapses into a pool of shadow and is incapacitated. He cannot reform. He is finished — not destroyed, but ended as an agent.
biography.director:
TACTICAL GUIDANCE:

  • This fight should take ~2 rounds if the party plays efficiently.
  • Dreyfus is dangerous (Elite) but exhausted in ways that show.
  • Round 1: Fights with precision and cruelty. Uses Wyrd-Read knowledge to target each hero's confidence. Shifts forms to scatter positioning.
  • Round 2: Villain Power depleted. Returns to human form. Faster, more direct — precision gone, replaced by pure violence. Most dangerous here.
  • At 20 Stamina: Stops shifting forms. He can't maintain the shapes. The party watches something degrade.
  • At 0 Stamina: Collapses. Flickering stops. For one moment, he looks completely human — not a shape-walker, but a person who has been doing something terrible for a very long time and has finally been stopped.

CALLBACK TO BEAT 9: Review what the party did to Dreyfus in Beat 9 — which PC wounded him, what weapon, where the blow landed. His degraded appearance here should carry the specific marks of that fight. If a PC drove a spear through his shoulder, his left arm doesn't shift cleanly. If they burned him, his raven form trails ash. The party should recognise their own handiwork.
